name: Havoc Dwarves
background:
Havoc Dwarves are those Dwarves who have embraced the Gods of Havoc. Their slow-moving soldiers are heavily armoured, supported by monstrous weapons and creatures created through dark rituals. 

The Havoc Dwarves’ ancestors were conquered by Orcs and abandoned by their former allies, and so they have embraced the Gods of Havoc. The Havoc Dwarves now seek to reclaim their ancient homes, building a new empire of blood and brutality so that they might never be humiliated and conquered again.
backgroundFull:
The Dwarven tribes were once spread throughout Tyria, but as the plains were settled by humanity and Orcs came to the steppe, the Dwarves retreated into the mountains, building the Dwarfholds. The Dwarves’ ability to channel magic was limited compared to other species, so instead Dwarves became crafters and inventors, using runes to enchant items and imbue them with magic which their Rune Masters could later use.

When Havoc arrived in Tyria, the northern Dwarfholds were overrun by followers of Havoc, while the southern Dwarves joined the campaign against Havoc. Seeing their opportunity, the Orcs attacked and soon conquered the southern Dwarfholds. The southern Dwarves had a difficult life under the Orcs. The Orcs drove the Dwarves out of their holds and took whatever they wished from their subjects. The Dwarves held little status, reduced to performing hard labour in the fields and mines, and with their former allies too weakened to help, they lived under cruel Orc rule for generations.‍

When the Great Rift opened, it tore through much of the Dwarven lands. Many Orcs were killed by cave-ins, whilst the rest were forced to abandon the holds, leaving behind much of their armour and weapons, and the Dwarves rose up and drove away any Orcs who remained. Some Dwarves began to follow the Havoc Gods, claiming that it was Havoc who liberated them while their former allies abandoned them. These Havoc-worshipping Dwarves consolidated in the northern Dwarfholds.

When the Havoc Gods saw the lack of arcane potential in their Dwarven followers, their attention shifted to other mortal followers. While their gifts still appeared amongst the Havoc Dwarves, it was far more difficult for the Dwarves to catch the attention of their new patrons. In order to account for their lack of Magic, they began to use captives as conduits for powerful rituals and magic. Through this experimentation, they have discovered how to use blood magic to overcome their natural weakness. Some Havoc Dwarves have managed to catch the attention of their gods once more using this magic. However, Havoc Gods’ interest is ever-fleeting. 

To further impress the Havoc Gods, the Dwarves have used their mechanical knowledge to build more powerful war-machines, golems, and weapons. They do this in the vain hope that the blood they shed and the empires that they build may finally satisfy their aloof patrons. 

How will you appease the uncaring Gods?

spells.name: Rune of Piercing
spells.effect: Pick one friendly unit within 12", which gets Piercing Hunter once (next time the effect would apply).

parameters.name: effectDuration
parameters.value: once (next time the effect would apply)

costFormula.value: { name: params.effect.unitRule.name, rating: params.effect.unitRule.rating, multiplier: params.multiplier * (params.aura ? this.AuraSize : 1), avgUnit: !params.thisUnit }

effectDuration.name: once (next time the effect would apply)

spells.effectSkirmish: Pick one friendly unit within 12", which gets Piercing Hunter once (next time the effect would apply).

spells.effect: Pick one enemy model within 24", which takes 1 hits with AP(4).
spells.effectSkirmish: Pick one enemy model within 24", which takes 1 hits with AP(4).

specialRules.name: Fortified
specialRules.description: When units where all models have this rule take hits, those hits count as having AP(-1), to a min. of AP(0).

specialRules.name: Melee Shrouding Aura
specialRules.description: This model and up to 3 nearest friendly units picked before the start of the game get Melee Shrouding. If this model is killed, picked models that are deployed lose the rule.

specialRules.name: Sergeant
specialRules.description: When this model attacks, unmodified results of 6 to hit deal 1 extra hit (only the original hit counts as a 6 for special rules).

specialRules.name: Swift
specialRules.description: This model may ignore the Slow rule.

specialRules.name: Spell Conduit
specialRules.description: Casters within 12" that are from other friendly units may cast spells as if they were in this model's position, and get +1 to casting rolls when doing so.

specialRules.name: Armor
specialRules.description: Counts as having Defense X+.

specialRules.name: Havocbound Boost Aura
specialRules.description: This model and up to 3 nearest friendly units picked before the start of the game get Havocbound Boost. If this model is killed, picked models that are deployed lose the rule.

specialRules.name: Melee Slayer
specialRules.description: This model's melee weapons get AP(+2) against units where most models have Tough(12) or higher.

specialRules.name: Versatile Defense Aura
specialRules.description: This model and up to 3 nearest friendly units picked before the start of the game get Versatile Defense. If this model is killed, picked models that are deployed lose the rule.

specialRules.name: Havocbound
specialRules.description: When this model shoots at enemies over 9" away, or when it charges, its weapons get AP(+1).

specialRules.name: Swift Aura
specialRules.description: This model and up to 3 nearest friendly units picked before the start of the game get Swift. If this model is killed, picked models that are deployed lose the rule.

specialRules.name: Increased Shooting Range Aura
specialRules.description: This model and up to 3 nearest friendly units picked before the start of the game get +6" range when shooting. If this model is killed, picked models that are deployed lose the rule.

specialRules.name: Morale Debuff
specialRules.description: Once per activation, before attacking, pick up to 4 enemy units within 18", which gets -1 to morale test rolls once (next time the effect would apply).

specialRules.name: Melee Slayer Aura
specialRules.description: This model and up to 3 nearest friendly units picked before the start of the game get Melee Slayer. If this model is killed, picked models that are deployed lose the rule.

specialRules.name: Purge
specialRules.description: Ignores Regeneration, and against units where most models have Defense 2+ to Defense 4+, this weapon gets AP(+1).

specialRules.name: Relentless Mark
specialRules.description: Once per activation, before attacking, pick up to 4 enemy units within 18", which friendly units gets Relentless against once (next time the effect would apply).

specialRules.name: Steadfast Buff
specialRules.description: Once per activation, before attacking, pick up to 4 nearest friendly units within 12", which gets Steadfast once (next time the effect would apply).

specialRules.name: AP
specialRules.description: Targets get -X to Defense rolls when blocking hits.

specialRules.name: Melee Shrouding
specialRules.description: Enemies get -3" movement when trying to charge units where all models have this rule.

specialRules.name: Slow
specialRules.description: Moves -2" when using Advance, and -4” when using Rush/Charge.

specialRules.name: Caster
specialRules.description: Follows same rules as heroes, picking one random spell to cast, and spending all tokens to cast it when activated. If the chosen spell can’t be cast for any reason, then the model doesn’t cast.

specialRules.name: Defense
specialRules.description: This model gets +X to defense rolls.

specialRules.name: Havocbound Boost
specialRules.description: Models with Havocbound in this unit always get AP(+1) from Havocbound (instead of only when shooting over 9" away, or when charging).

specialRules.name: Tough
specialRules.description: Once a model has taken as many wounds as its Tough value, it is killed (remove from play).

specialRules.name: Versatile Defense
specialRules.description: When a unit where all models have this rule is activated, pick one effect: the unit either gets +1 to defense rolls, or enemy units get -1 to hit rolls against it. This only applies once (next time the effect would apply) and only lasts until the units' next activation.

specialRules.name: Slayer
specialRules.description: This model's weapons get AP(+2) against units where most models have Tough(3) or higher.

specialRules.name: Regeneration
specialRules.description: When a unit where all models have this rule takes wounds, roll one die for each. On a 5+ it is ignored.

specialRules.name: Ignores Regeneration
specialRules.description: This weapon ignores Regeneration.

specialRules.name: Relentless
specialRules.description: When this model shoots at enemies over 9" away, unmodified results of 6 to hit deal 1 extra hit (only the original hit counts as a 6 for special rules).

specialRules.name: Steadfast
specialRules.description: If a unit where all models have this rule is Shaken at the beginning of the round, roll one die. On a 4+, it stops being Shaken.

specialRules.name: Piercing Hunter
specialRules.description: This model gets AP(+1) when shooting enemies over 9" away.

specialRules.name: Blast
specialRules.description: Ignores cover, and after resolving other special rules, each hit is multiplied by X, where X is up to as many hits as enemy models in the target unit and within 3” of it. Hits must be split evenly between all enemy units within 3” of any model from the target (players pick how).
